title: Rich Smoky Rye Bake
description: A comforting Malaysian-inspired dish featuring smoky rye, perfect for a cozy evening. No onions or garlic in sight!

ingredients.name: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: optional, omit for a vegetarian version

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: use a low-sodium version if needed

instructions: Cut the rye bread into 1-inch (2.5 cm) cubes and place them in a large mixing bowl.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter over low heat. Add the miso paste and whisk until smooth.
Pour the miso-butter mixture over the rye bread and toss until the bread is evenly coated.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, egg, grated ginger, chili flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the egg mixture over the rye bread mixture and toss until the bread is well coated.
Transfer the mixture to a 6-cup (1.4 L) copper mug or a 6-cup (1.4 L) baking dish.
Bake the rye bake for 35-40 minutes, or until it's golden brown and the bread is crispy on the outside.
Remove the rye bake from the oven and let it cool for 5-10 minutes.
Garnish with chopped cilantro, if desired. Serve warm and enjoy!
Note: If using a copper mug, be careful when removing it from the oven as it may be hot.
If the rye bake is too crumbly, you can try baking it for an additional 5-10 minutes.
